steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: The first sentence states that Peru lowered the voting age from 21 to 18. The second sentence states that many young people gained a new opportunity to vote. The second sentence is a direct result of the first — this is a cause-and-effect relationship, so the transition must signal that the second event follows from the first.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Similarly,': signals that two situations are comparable or parallel, but the second sentence is a consequence of the first, not a parallel case. ✗
  • Choice B — 'By contrast,': signals opposition between two ideas, but young people gaining the vote is not in opposition to the voting age being lowered — it follows from it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Consequently,': signals that one event results directly from another, which matches the cause-and-effect relationship here exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Nevertheless,': signals an unexpected outcome despite an obstacle, but nothing in the passage works against young people gaining the vote. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
